name: Changelog.md Workmanship
description: Build exhaustive, agent-friendly CHANGELOG.md files from git history, releases, and issue trackers. Use when writing, rebuilding, or researching project history for changelogs or release timelines.

references.description: One-page cheatsheet with non-negotiables, best default structure, and the most common failure modes.
Step-by-step process for reconstructing project history, chunking large histories, and incrementally updating the live changelog.
Copy-paste markdown skeletons for changelog openings, version timelines, capability-wave sections, agent notes, and research memos.
Rules for release links, tag links, commit URLs, issue-tracker links, date handling, and evidence hierarchy.
Exact prompts for narrow changelog updates, medium project rewrites, and full-history chunked reconstructions.
Distilled patterns from strong changelog work: version spine plus themes, issue tracker as intent layer, compaction-resistant workflows, and separation of release notes from canonical history.
Common changelog failure modes and how to recover when histories are too large, sources disagree, or the draft still reads like fluff or a diff dump.
Quality thresholds for small updates, standard rebuilds, and large-history reconstructions, including signs a changelog is actually strong.
Concrete git, gh, jq, and skill-script command recipes for building the version spine, chunking research, mining tracker history, and auditing the finished changelog.
Normalization rules and workflows for beads, GitHub Issues, Linear/Jira-style exports, and milestone markdown so tracker evidence can be folded into a durable changelog cleanly.
Multi-pass operating model for huge repositories with chunk files, coverage ledgers, staged drafting, and explicit context-management discipline.

sources.description: Patterns extracted while rebuilding the full-history beads_rust CHANGELOG.md with accurate release-vs-tag distinctions, direct commit links, and scoped beads-history links.
